hard_constraints: Do not change time stop 12 days.
Do not open parallel redesign tracks.
Do not reactivate narrow_confirmation_entry in this phase.
Do not change the official baseline.

narrow_confirmation_definition.what_narrow_confirmation_means: Narrow confirmation means a newly started base entry signal must persist for one additional trading bar before entry is allowed.

measurement_variables.usage: Defines the base signal run and the new-signal anchor.
True on the first bar where phase_1_signal_layer1 changes from false to true.
True only when the next trading bar after confirmation_anchor_start still has phase_1_signal_layer1 == true.

narrow_confirmation_definition.pass_rule: A signal passes narrow confirmation only if phase_1_signal_layer1 is true on the first follow-through bar immediately after a new-signal anchor, and entry becomes eligible on that confirmation bar.
narrow_confirmation_definition.fail_rule: A signal fails narrow confirmation if the follow-through bar immediately after a new-signal anchor does not keep phase_1_signal_layer1 true.
narrow_confirmation_definition.pipeline_touchpoint: Apply the confirmation gate after the base signal run is detected from phase_1_signal_layer1 and before any trade is sent to Layer 4 sizing or Layer 5 exit handling.
what_does_not_change: Layer 1 regime policy does not change.
Layer 3 remains optional default off and is not part of this hypothesis.
Layer 5 ATR14 x 2.5, trailing logic, and time stop 12 days do not change.
No additional eligibility gate or regime filter is introduced.
entry_side_boundary: This gate only checks one-bar persistence of the existing base signal.
It does not redefine market regime.
It does not introduce cross-sectional or absolute eligibility redesign.
It does not combine with freshness or any other additional filter.

allowed_variation_space.frozen_first_execution_rule: entry only after one-bar persistence confirmation of phase_1_signal_layer1
notes: This definition remains archived as a failed branch for this phase.
No combined redesign with regime, eligibility, freshness, or exit changes is authorized.
No reactivation is authorized without a new governance decision.

disallowed_changes: Combined redesign with any other gate or filter.
sequencing_rules: Narrow confirmation is closed and archived for this phase.
Only one hypothesis family may be active at a time.
Do not reactivate narrow_confirmation_entry without a new governance decision.
Do not combine two redesigns in one experiment pass.
exit_criteria: Narrow confirmation remains documented explicitly as a historical failed branch.
The failed branch status is frozen without ambiguity.
The roadmap no longer points to narrow-confirmation execution setup.
Paper trading remains blocked and retest remains blocked.
